title: optparse module is deprecated
message: The optparse module was deprecated in Python 2.7 and removed in Python 3. It has been superseded by argparse, which provides a more powerful and flexible command-line argument parsing interface.
suggestion: Use `import argparse` instead. The argparse module provides the same functionality with a more consistent API, automatic help generation, and better error messages.

title: commands module is deprecated
message: The commands module was deprecated in Python 2.6 and removed in Python 3. It was used for running shell commands.
suggestion: Use the `subprocess` module instead. `subprocess.check_output()` or `subprocess.run()` provide the same functionality with better security and more control.

title: urllib2 is a Python 2 module
message: urllib2 was part of Python 2's standard library and was removed in Python 3. Its functionality was reorganized into urllib.request, urllib.error, and urllib.parse.
suggestion: Use `urllib.request` from Python 3's standard library. For example: `urllib.request.urlopen(url)` instead of `urllib2.urlopen(url)`.

title: httplib is a Python 2 module
message: httplib was part of Python 2's standard library and was removed in Python 3. It was renamed to http.client in Python 3.
suggestion: Use `http.client` from Python 3's standard library instead. For higher-level HTTP, use `requests` or `urllib.request`.

title: urlparse is a Python 2 module
message: urlparse was part of Python 2's standard library and was removed in Python 3. Its functionality was moved to urllib.parse.
suggestion: Use `from urllib.parse import urlparse, urljoin, urlencode` in Python 3 instead.

title: imp module is deprecated
message: The imp module was deprecated in Python 3.4 and removed in Python 3.12. It was the original import internals module.
suggestion: Use `importlib` instead. The `importlib` module provides all the functionality of imp with a cleaner API, including `importlib.import_module()` and `importlib.util` for loader operations.

title: collections.Callable is deprecated
message: collections.Callable was deprecated in Python 3.3 and removed in Python 3.9. It was an abstract base class used for checking callable objects.
suggestion: Use `collections.abc.Callable` instead: `from collections.abc import Callable`. Or use `callable()` built-in function for simple checks.

title: collections.Iterable is deprecated
message: collections.Iterable was deprecated in Python 3.3 and removed in Python 3.9. Abstract base classes should now be imported from collections.abc.

title: asynchat module is deprecated
message: The asynchat module was deprecated in Python 3.6 and removed in Python 3.12. It was part of the legacy asyncore framework and is not compatible with asyncio.
suggestion: Use the modern `asyncio` library for async I/O operations. For protocol handling, use `asyncio.Protocol` or `asyncio.StreamReader`/`StreamWriter`.

title: asyncore module is deprecated
message: The asyncore module was deprecated in Python 3.6 and removed in Python 3.12. It was a legacy async I/O framework that predates asyncio.
suggestion: Use the `asyncio` module instead. It provides the same async socket handling with a modern, well-maintained implementation.

title: cgi module is deprecated
message: The cgi module was deprecated in Python 3.11 and removed in Python 3.13. It was used for CGI scripting but the CGI standard itself is considered legacy.
suggestion: Use a modern web framework (Flask, FastAPI, Django) instead of CGI scripts. For multipart form parsing, use `cgi.FieldStorage` alternatives from the framework's request object.

title: cgitb module is deprecated
message: The cgitb module was deprecated in Python 3.11 and removed in Python 3.13 alongside the cgi module.
suggestion: Use proper logging and debugging tools instead of CGI traceback utilities. Use the `logging` module with stack trace capture, or a proper debugger.

title: pipes module is deprecated
message: The pipes module was deprecated in Python 3.11 and removed in Python 3.13. It was used for creating shell pipeline templates.
suggestion: Use `subprocess` module with pipes directly. `subprocess.run('cmd1 | cmd2', shell=True)` or use `subprocess.PIPE` for programmatic piping.

title: distutils module is deprecated
message: The distutils module was deprecated in Python 3.10 and removed in Python 3.12. It was the original packaging and distribution tool.
suggestion: Use `setuptools` instead, or use the modern `build` and `twine` tools for package publishing. For configuration, use `pyproject.toml` with `setuptools` or `flit`.

title: distutils.version is deprecated
message: distutils.version.LooseVersion and StrictVersion were deprecated alongside distutils in Python 3.10 and removed in Python 3.12.
suggestion: Use `packaging.version.Version` and `packaging.version.parse()` from the `packaging` library (available as `pip install packaging`).

title: collections.MutableMapping is deprecated
message: collections.MutableMapping was deprecated in Python 3.3 and removed in Python 3.9. Abstract base classes should be imported from collections.abc.

title: string module constants are deprecated as module attributes
message: String constants like string.letters, string.lowercase, string.uppercase were deprecated in Python 3.0. They moved to the `string` module constants but many remain as compatibility aliases.
suggestion: Use explicit string constants like `string.ascii_letters`, `string.ascii_lowercase`, `string.ascii_uppercase`, `string.digits`, and `string.hexdigits`.
